task_completion.description: Verify that the agent injected the event into the Graphite Events API.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a terminal window showing an HTTP POST request (e.g., using 'curl') to 'http://localhost/events/' with a JSON payload containing '"what": "Cache Flush"' and '"tags": "mitigation_applied"'.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the Graphite dashboard with the correct name.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Graphite web interface and confirm the dashboard title is exactly 'Sev1 Mitigation Review'.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Database Load' graph is created with the correct metric.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a graph titled 'Database Load' in the dashboard. Its legend or configuration must show the target 'movingAverage(servers.rds_database.cpu.utilization, 10)'.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Frontend Traffic' graph is created with the correct metric.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a graph titled 'Frontend Traffic' in the dashboard. Its legend or configuration must show the target 'servers.load_balancer.requests.count'.

task_completion.description: Verify both graphs include the event target and display the event marker.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the target 'events("mitigation_applied")' in the legends or configurations of both graphs. Additionally, an event marker (such as a vertical line or annotation) should be visible on the graphs indicating the injected event.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual Graphite web interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show a real web browser navigating the Graphite UI, not a mockup or a text editor with HTML.

integrity.description: Verify the event injection was performed via a real HTTP request.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for actual execution of a command like 'curl' or a script in a terminal, rather than just typing the command without running it.

integrity.description: Verify the dashboard was not faked using browser developer tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure there are no browser developer tools (Inspect Element) open that are being used to alter the text of the dashboard title or graph legends.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected dashboard name is exactly 'Sev1 Mitigation Review'. The injected event must have the 'what' field set to 'Cache Flush' and the 'tags' field set to 'mitigation_applied'.
